My ‘pendix6 pains each time it rains.My nose is cold, my toes are numb.I have a sliver7 in my thumb. My neck is stiff, my voice is weak, I hardly whisper when I speak. My tongue is filling up my mouth, I think my hair is falling out. My elbow’s bent, my spine ain’t straight, My temperature is one-o-eight. My brain is shrunk, I cannot hear, There is a hole inside my ear.I have a hangnail8, and my heart is--what?What’s that? What’s that you say?You say today is. . .Saturday?G’bye, I’m going out to play!”The India BookmarkIn traditional Indian folk theatre, comic characters were often called Vidushakas. They entertained audiences by complaining dramatically about small problems and making people laugh at human weaknesses.pendix: short form of a small organ inside the body- appendixsliver: a small, thin, sharp piece of woodhangnail: a small torn piece of skin near a fingernail-Shel Silversteincircle of the correct answer in the OMR sheet. Why does the girl say she cannot go to school at the beginning of the poem?She lost her books 	(b) She is feeling very sick   	(c) She missed the bus 	(d) She has no homeworkWhich two illnesses are mentioned in the first line of complaints?Fever and cough   	(b) Measles and mumps   	(c) Cold and headache   (d) Rash and flu  	3. How many chicken pox does Peggy first count? 	(a) Fourteen 	(b) Fifteen 	(c) Sixteen 	(d) Seventeen 	4. What temperature does Peggy claim to have? 	(a) 100 	(b) 102 	(c) 104 	(d) 108Complete the following lines. __________________________, 	2. My nose is cold, _____________. 	 A gash, a rash and _____________. 	 _____________________________. 	 ______________________________ 	 _______________, my voice is weak,  	 I’m going blind _________________. 	 _____________________________Answer the following questions with reference to the 	CG:2, C:2.2	 context.	Critical Thinking“I cannot go to school today, 	 Said little Peggy Ann McKay.”  	 a. Name the poem and the poet.Who is speaking these lines?What does this line suggest about her situation?“What’s that? What’s that you say? 	 You say today is… Saturday?”What causes Peggy to stop complaining?What change do we notice in her? Why?What is the message conveyed through this moment?	Put on Your Thinking Caps	 	/A limerick is a nonsensical and funny verse consisting of five lines. Study the limerick given below and identify the rhyme scheme.There was an Old Man with a beard, Who said, “It is just as I feared! Two Owls and a Hen, Four Larks and a Wren, Have all built their nests in my beard!”Follow the structure given below and write a limerick on your own.Line 1:Introduction of a person or placeThere was child from ________________Lines 2-4:A silly storyWho _____________________________ _________________________________Line 5:An unexpected ending__________________________________________________________________	Dictionary & Vocabulary Skills	  	/Use a dictionary to find the meanings of these words. Then sort them into the correct column.•   fever       • imagination       • symptom       • illness       • excuse      • conditionRelated to HealthRelated to Thoughts/IdeasEach sentence contains a wrongly spelt word related to health, feelings or excuses. Identify them and write the correct spelling in the given space. 	1. He gave a very convinsing excuse to avoid school.  ______________________ 	2. The doctor checked him carefully to find the illnes.  ______________________ 	3. She suddenly felt enerjetic after hearing the news.  ______________________ 	4. His behaviour seemed quite unusal that day.  ______________________ 	5. The child tried to appear very serios and weak.  ______________________ 	6. The teacher quickly understood the situashun.  ______________________Scan & LearnScan the QR code to access Ask questions, clear yout the worksheet for this doubts, and learn more with chapter.AI.
